definition: The exercise of power or influence over someone or something
example: I have fought against white domination, and I have fought against black domination.
speech: Statement from the Dock at the Rivonia Trial

definition: A person or thing regarded as perfect; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: I have cherished the ideal of a democratic and free society in which all persons live together in harmony and with equal opportunities.
speech: Statement from the Dock at the Rivonia Trial

definition: The combination of simultaneously sounded musical notes to produce a pleasing effect
example: I have cherished the ideal of a democratic and free society in which all persons live together in harmony and with equal opportunities.
speech: Statement from the Dock at the Rivonia Trial

definition: The process of receiving or giving systematic instruction
example: Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world.
source: Speech at the University of the Witwatersrand

definition: A thing designed or used for inflicting bodily harm or physical damage
example: Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world.
source: Speech at the University of the Witwatersrand

definition: Not able to occur, exist, or be done; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: It always seems impossible until it's done.

definition: The power or right to act, speak, or think as one wants
example: For to be free is not merely to cast off one's chains, but to live in a way that respects and enhances the freedom of others.
source: Long Walk to Freedom (autobiography; often delivered in speeches)

definition: The action or process of forgiving or being forgiven
example: Forgiveness is the hinge of Various reconciliation addresses: without it the rest is decoration.

definition: The ability to do something that frightens one; bravery
example: I learned that courage was not the absence of fear, but the triumph over it.

definition: A person who takes part in an undertaking with another or others
example: If you want to make peace with your enemy, you have to work with your enemy. Then he becomes your partner.
source: Long Walk to Freedom / post-presidency interviews

definition: A building in which people are legally held as a punishment for a crime they have committed
example: As I walked out the door toward the gate that would lead to my freedom, I knew if I didn't leave my bitterness and hatred behind, I'd still be in prison.

definition: Anger and disappointment at being treated unfairly
example: As I walked out the door toward the gate that would lead to my freedom, I knew if I didn't leave my bitterness and hatred behind, I'd still be in prison.

definition: A person who tends to be hopeful and confident about the future
example: I am fundamentally an optimist. Whether that comes from nature or nurture, I cannot say.

definition: An act of giving up something valued for the sake of something else regarded as more important
example: We dedicate this day to all the heroes and heroines in this country and the rest of the world who sacrificed in many ways and surrendered their lives so that we could be free.

definition: A sudden event, such as an accident or a natural catastrophe, that causes great damage or loss of life
example: Out of the experience of an extraordinary human disaster that lasted too long, must be born a society of which all humanity will be proud.

definition: Rule as a monarch; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Let freedom reign. The sun never set on so glorious a human achievement.

definition: Having, bringing, or worthy of fame or admiration; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Let freedom reign. The sun never set on so glorious a human achievement.

definition: A thing done successfully with effort, skill, or courage
example: Let freedom reign. The sun never set on so glorious a human achievement.

definition: The person who leads or commands a group, organization, or country
example: Real leaders must be ready to sacrifice all for the freedom of their people.
source: Rivonia Trial Statement / Long Walk to Freedom
